name: Type-I seesaw
summary: Adds heavy right-handed neutrinos N_R with Majorana mass M_R >> v. Block-diagonalising the resulting 6x6 mass matrix yields light neutrino masses m_nu ~ y_nu^2 v^2 / M_R, naturally tiny for M_R near the GUT scale. The mechanism turns the neutrino-mass smallness from a Yukawa fine-tuning into a high-scale mass-ratio puzzle with a clear UV interpretation.

addresses_puzzles.note: For y_nu ~ y_t and M_R ~ 10^14 GeV, light masses naturally come out at the observed eV-scale. The 'pattern' is the m_nu ~ m_D^2 / M_R compression that turns three small numbers into a single high-scale ratio.

touches_benchmarks.note: Type-I seesaw is the textbook mechanism that produces this benchmark's compression ansatz m_nu ~ y_nu^2 v^2 / M_R.
Majorana mass for active neutrinos generates 0nubb at a rate set by m_betabeta; current experiments probe inverted hierarchy.

introduces.note: Three Majorana mass eigenvalues plus a complex 3x3 Yukawa matrix; with PMNS observables most parameters are fitted to data, leaving ~3 free scales (the M_R hierarchy).

history: Minkowski (1977); Yanagida (1979); Gell-Mann, Ramond, Slansky (1979); Mohapatra and Senjanovic (1980). Type-II (scalar triplet) and type-III (fermion triplet) variants followed. The seesaw is the textbook explanation; its high-scale character makes direct verification difficult, but neutrinoless double beta decay and leptogenesis provide indirect probes.

references.citation: Minkowski, 'mu -> e gamma at a rate of one out of 10^9 muon decays?', Phys. Lett. B 67, 421 (1977)
Mohapatra and Senjanovic, 'Neutrino mass and spontaneous parity violation', PRL 44, 912 (1980)
